ability.simple_definition: a skill or power to do something

She was tired, yet she kept on walking.
Is this bag yours?
The final decision is yours.
The temperature dropped to zero last night.
He scored zero on the first question but did well on the rest.

absolutely.simple_definition: completely; with no doubt at all
examples: I'm absolutely certain I left my keys on the table.

academic.simple_definition: relating to education or studying at a school or university
examples: She has a strong academic record and hopes to study medicine.

access.simple_definition: the right or ability to use or enter something
examples: All students have access to the library during opening hours.

accommodation.simple_definition: a place where someone lives or stays temporarily
examples: The university provides accommodation for first-year students.

account.simple_definition: a record of money received and spent; to give an explanation of something
examples: He opened a bank account before starting his new job.

achievement.simple_definition: something successfully completed through effort or skill
examples: Finishing the marathon was a great personal achievement.

ad.simple_definition: a short form of advertisement; a notice promoting something
examples: I saw an ad for the new film on the bus this morning.

addition.simple_definition: something added; the process of adding
examples: In addition to the main course, the restaurant also offers three desserts.

admire.simple_definition: to respect and approve of someone or something
examples: I really admire her for the way she handled such a difficult situation.

admit.simple_definition: to agree that something is true; to allow someone to enter
examples: He admitted that he had forgotten to do his homework.

advanced.simple_definition: at a high level; developed beyond a basic stage
examples: This textbook is designed for advanced learners of English.

advise.simple_definition: to recommend something; to give someone your opinion about what they should do
examples: The doctor advised her to rest for at least three days.

afford.simple_definition: to have enough money to pay for something
examples: They could not afford to buy a new car, so they repaired the old one.

age.simple_definition: the number of years a person has lived; a period of history
examples: At the age of eighteen, he left home for the first time.
The aged professor had taught at the university for over forty years.

agent.simple_definition: a person who acts on behalf of another; a representative
examples: The actor hired an agent to manage his contracts.

agreement.simple_definition: a shared understanding between two or more people or groups
examples: The two companies finally reached an agreement after weeks of talks.

ahead.simple_definition: in front; in the future; in a better position
examples: There is a long road ahead, but we are ready for it.

aim.simple_definition: to intend to do or achieve something; a goal or purpose
examples: The programme aims to help young people find work after leaving school.

alarm.simple_definition: a device that warns of danger; a feeling of fear or worry
examples: The smoke alarm went off in the middle of the night and woke everyone up.

album.simple_definition: a collection of music tracks released together; a book for storing photos
examples: The band released their third album last month and it reached number one.

alcohol.simple_definition: drinks such as wine and beer that can make people drunk
examples: The doctor told him to stop drinking alcohol for at least six weeks.

alcoholic.simple_definition: a person who cannot control their drinking; containing alcohol
examples: There are many support groups available for people who are alcoholic.
She was amazed when she found out she had won the first prize.

ambition.simple_definition: a strong desire to achieve something, especially success
examples: His ambition was to become the best chef in the country.

ambitious.simple_definition: having a strong desire to succeed; requiring a lot of effort
examples: She is very ambitious and works extremely hard to reach her goals.

analyse.simple_definition: to examine something in detail to understand or explain it
examples: Scientists will analyse the water samples to check for pollution.

analysis.simple_definition: a careful examination of something to understand it better
examples: The report includes a detailed analysis of the company's financial performance.

announce.simple_definition: to make a public statement about something
examples: The headteacher announced that the school would close early on Friday.

announcement.simple_definition: a public or official statement about something
examples: An announcement was made over the speakers asking passengers to wait.

annoy.simple_definition: to make someone feel slightly angry or irritated
examples: The noise from the road works began to annoy the people living nearby.
She was very annoyed when she realised she had missed her train again.
It is really annoying when people talk loudly in a quiet library.

apart.simple_definition: separated by a space or distance; not together
examples: The two houses are only a few metres apart from each other.

apologize.simple_definition: to say sorry for something you have done wrong
examples: He apologized to his friend for arriving so late to the meeting.

application.simple_definition: a formal request for a job or place on a course; a computer program
examples: She sent her application for the position by email last Tuesday.

appointment.simple_definition: an arrangement to meet someone at a specific time
examples: I have a doctor's appointment at ten o'clock tomorrow morning.

appreciate.simple_definition: to understand the value of something; to be grateful for something
examples: I really appreciate all the help you gave me during that difficult time.

approximately.simple_definition: close to a particular number but not exactly
examples: The journey takes approximately two hours by train.

arrest.simple_definition: to take someone to a police station because they may have broken the law
examples: The police arrested the man and took him in for questioning.

arrival.simple_definition: the act of reaching a place; a person who has just come
examples: Please check in at the desk upon arrival at the hotel.

assignment.simple_definition: a piece of work given to a student or employee to complete
examples: The teacher gave the class a writing assignment to finish by Friday.
A member of staff will be happy to assist you with your luggage.

atmosphere.simple_definition: the air around the Earth; the general feeling or mood of a place
examples: The restaurant had a warm and relaxed atmosphere that made guests feel at home.

attach.simple_definition: to fasten or connect one thing to another
examples: Please attach a copy of your CV when you submit the application form.

attitude.simple_definition: a way of thinking or feeling about something or someone
examples: She has a very positive attitude and always tries to see the good in things.

attract.simple_definition: to make someone interested in or want to come to something
examples: The new museum has already attracted thousands of visitors since it opened.

attraction.simple_definition: something that draws people's interest; a place people want to visit
examples: The old castle is the main tourist attraction in the town.

authority.simple_definition: the power to make decisions or give orders; an official body
examples: The local authority is responsible for managing the public parks.

aware.simple_definition: knowing about or conscious of something
examples: Are you aware that the meeting has been moved to Thursday?

backwards.simple_definition: towards the back; in the opposite direction to normal
examples: He took two steps backwards to get a better view of the painting.

bake.simple_definition: to cook food in an oven using dry heat
examples: She decided to bake a cake for her friend's birthday celebration.

balance.simple_definition: to keep things equal or stable; the amount of money in an account
examples: It can be hard to keep a good balance between work and personal life.

ban.simple_definition: to officially say that something is not allowed; an official rule
examples: The school introduced a ban on mobile phones in classrooms.

base.simple_definition: the lowest or main part of something; to use as a starting point
examples: The company is based in the capital city, with offices in six other countries.

basic.simple_definition: forming the simplest or most important part; essential
examples: A basic knowledge of grammar is essential for learning a new language.

basis.simple_definition: the main reason or principle behind something; how something is organised
examples: Staff are paid on a weekly basis and receive their money every Friday.

battery.simple_definition: a device that provides electrical power
examples: The remote control stopped working because it needed new batteries.

battle.simple_definition: a fight between two groups; a difficult struggle
examples: The soldiers were preparing for a long and difficult battle ahead.

beauty.simple_definition: the quality of being attractive; a person or thing that is attractive
examples: The beauty of the mountain landscape took everyone's breath away.

bee.simple_definition: a flying insect that makes honey and can sting
examples: There was a bee in the kitchen, so she opened the window to let it out.

belief.simple_definition: the feeling of being certain that something is true; an accepted principle
examples: It is my strong belief that everyone deserves the chance to learn.

bell.simple_definition: a metal object that makes a ringing sound when hit
examples: The bell rang to signal the end of the school day.

bend.simple_definition: to change from a straight line to a curved shape; a curve in a road
examples: The road bends sharply to the left just after the petrol station.

bite.simple_definition: to cut or hold something with your teeth; a small amount of food
examples: He was bitten by a dog while walking in the park.

block.simple_definition: a solid piece of material; a group of buildings; to prevent movement
examples: There is a large apartment block being built at the end of our street.

bomb.simple_definition: an explosive device; to attack a place with bombs
examples: The city was bombed heavily during the second year of the war.

border.simple_definition: the line that divides two countries or areas
examples: We crossed the border into the next country just before midnight.

bother.simple_definition: to cause trouble or annoyance; to take trouble to do something
examples: Don't bother cooking tonight — I will bring something home for dinner.

branch.simple_definition: a part of a tree; a local office of a company
examples: The bank has branches in over twenty cities across the country.

brand.simple_definition: a product made by a well-known company; a mark to identify something
examples: This brand of coffee is very popular because of its strong flavour.

brave.simple_definition: ready to face danger without fear; showing courage
examples: It was very brave of her to speak up about what she had witnessed.

breath.simple_definition: the air that goes in and out of your body; a single act of breathing
examples: He took a deep breath before walking into the examination room.

breathe.simple_definition: to take air into your lungs and let it out
examples: It was so smoky in the room that it was hard to breathe properly.

breathing.simple_definition: the process of taking air in and out of the body
examples: His breathing was slow and steady, which showed he was very calm.

bride.simple_definition: a woman on her wedding day
examples: The bride looked beautiful as she walked into the church.

bubble.simple_definition: a small ball of air inside a liquid; to produce bubbles
examples: The soup began to bubble as it heated up on the stove.

bury.simple_definition: to put a dead body in the ground; to hide something underground
examples: They buried the time capsule in the school garden.

calm.simple_definition: peaceful and quiet; not excited or angry; to make someone feel relaxed
examples: He stayed completely calm under pressure, which impressed everyone.

campaign.simple_definition: a planned series of actions to achieve a goal
examples: The charity launched a campaign to raise money for clean water projects.

campus.simple_definition: the grounds and buildings of a university or college
examples: The university campus has its own shops and sports facilities.

candidate.simple_definition: a person who applies for a job or is being considered for something
examples: There were three candidates being interviewed for the same position.

cap.simple_definition: a flat hat; a lid or top; an upper limit
examples: He wore a blue cap and a light jacket to the outdoor concert.

captain.simple_definition: the leader of a team or ship; an officer of a certain rank
examples: The team captain gave a motivating speech before the final match.

careless.simple_definition: not giving enough attention to what you are doing
examples: A careless mistake in the calculations led to a serious problem.

category.simple_definition: a group of things that share similar features
examples: The film won in the best drama category at the international awards.

ceiling.simple_definition: the top surface inside a room, above your head
examples: The ceiling in the old church was decorated with beautiful painted figures.

celebration.simple_definition: a special event done to mark a happy occasion
examples: The whole family came together for a big celebration on their anniversary.

central.simple_definition: in or near the middle; most important
examples: The hotel is in a very central location, five minutes from the train station.

centre.simple_definition: the middle point of something; an important place for an activity
examples: The town centre is full of shops, cafes, and restaurants.

ceremony.simple_definition: a formal event held to mark a special occasion
examples: The graduation ceremony was held in the main hall of the university.

chain.simple_definition: a series of connected metal links; a series of related things
examples: She wore a thin gold chain around her neck.

challenge.simple_definition: something difficult that requires effort; to question something
examples: Learning to drive in a busy city was a real challenge for her.

champion.simple_definition: a person who has won a competition; to support a cause
examples: He became the national swimming champion at the age of just sixteen.

channel.simple_definition: a narrow body of water; a television or radio station
examples: She switched to a different channel because there was nothing interesting on.

chapter.simple_definition: a section of a book; a part of someone's life or history
examples: Please read the first two chapters before the next lesson.

charge.simple_definition: to ask for payment; to accuse someone of a crime
examples: The hotel charges extra for parking in its underground car park.

cheap.simple_definition: low in price; not costing much money
examples: The market sells fresh fruit and vegetables at very cheap prices.

cheat.simple_definition: to act dishonestly to get an advantage; a person who cheats
examples: He was caught cheating in the exam and was asked to leave the room.

cheerful.simple_definition: happy and positive in mood; bringing a feeling of happiness
examples: She is always cheerful in the mornings, which makes her a pleasant colleague.

chemical.simple_definition: a substance used or made in a chemical process; relating to chemistry
examples: Some chemical products should not be mixed as they can be dangerous.

chest.simple_definition: the front part of the body between the neck and stomach; a large box
examples: She felt a sharp pain in her chest and went immediately to see a doctor.

childhood.simple_definition: the period of your life when you are a child
examples: She had a happy childhood, growing up near the countryside.

claim.simple_definition: to say that something is true; to ask for something you have a right to
examples: He claimed that he had been waiting for over an hour when the bus arrived.

clause.simple_definition: a part of a legal document; a group of words with a subject and verb
examples: The contract included a clause about what would happen if either side broke it.

click.simple_definition: to make a short sharp sound; to press a button on a computer mouse
examples: Double-click on the icon to open the programme on your screen.

client.simple_definition: a person who uses the services of a professional
examples: The lawyer met with her client to discuss the details of the case.

climb.simple_definition: to go up something using hands and feet; to rise to a higher position
examples: The children loved to climb the old oak tree in the garden.

cloth.simple_definition: a piece of fabric; material used for making clothes
examples: She used a clean cloth to wipe the spilt milk from the table.

clue.simple_definition: a piece of information that helps you solve a problem or mystery
examples: The police found an important clue at the scene of the crime.

coal.simple_definition: a hard black mineral used as fuel
examples: The old mine produced coal for over a century before it was finally closed.

coin.simple_definition: a small piece of metal used as money
examples: She found an old coin in the garden while digging up the flower bed.

collection.simple_definition: a group of things gathered together; money collected for charity
examples: The museum has an impressive collection of art from the sixteenth century.

coloured.simple_definition: having a colour other than black or white
examples: The children drew coloured pictures to decorate the classroom walls.

combine.simple_definition: to join two or more things together to make one
examples: This recipe combines sweet and sour flavours in a very interesting way.

commercial.simple_definition: related to buying and selling; an advertisement on television or radio
examples: The area has become very commercial, with new shops opening every month.

commit.simple_definition: to do something wrong; to promise to do something
examples: She committed herself fully to the project and worked very long hours.

communication.simple_definition: the process of sharing information or ideas between people
examples: Good communication between team members is essential for any project.

comparison.simple_definition: the act of looking at two or more things to find similarities or differences
examples: A comparison of the two products shows that one is cheaper but less durable.

competitive.simple_definition: involving competition; wanting very much to win
examples: The job market is very competitive, so you need a strong CV to stand out.

competitor.simple_definition: a person or company that competes with others
examples: The new sports brand quickly became a serious competitor for established companies.

complaint.simple_definition: an expression of dissatisfaction about something
examples: The hotel received a complaint about the noise from the room above.

complex.simple_definition: made of many connected parts; difficult to understand or explain
examples: The new software has a complex set of features that takes time to learn.

concentrate.simple_definition: to focus your attention on something
examples: It is hard to concentrate when there is a lot of noise in the background.

conclude.simple_definition: to reach a decision after thinking carefully; to bring something to an end
examples: The report concludes that more funding is needed for local health services.

conclusion.simple_definition: the final part of something; a decision made after thinking about the facts
examples: At the end of the meeting, everyone reached the same conclusion.

confident.simple_definition: having trust in yourself or your abilities; showing certainty
examples: She walked into the interview feeling confident and well prepared.

confirm.simple_definition: to show that something is definitely true; to say arrangements are fixed

confuse.simple_definition: to make someone unable to understand something; to mix things up

confused.simple_definition: unable to understand something clearly; not sure about what is happening
examples: He looked confused when she explained the new rules for the second time.

connection.simple_definition: a link between two or more things

consequence.simple_definition: something that happens as a result of an action; an effect or outcome

consist.simple_definition: to be made up of certain parts or elements

consume.simple_definition: to eat or drink something; to use up energy or resources

consumer.simple_definition: a person who buys goods or uses services

contact.simple_definition: to communicate with someone; a person you know through work

container.simple_definition: a box, bottle, or other object used to hold something

content.simple_definition: the things contained in something; feeling satisfied with what you have

continuous.simple_definition: happening without stopping or without interruption

contrast.simple_definition: a clear difference between two things; to show differences

convenient.simple_definition: easy to use or suited to your needs; not causing difficulty

convince.simple_definition: to make someone believe something; to persuade someone to do something

cool.simple_definition: slightly cold; fashionable; to become or make colder
examples: The weather was cool and fresh, which made the walk very enjoyable.

costume.simple_definition: clothes worn for a performance or to represent a historical period

cottage.simple_definition: a small house, usually in the country

cotton.simple_definition: a natural plant material used to make fabric

countryside.simple_definition: land outside towns and cities, used for farming or left wild

court.simple_definition: a place where legal cases are heard; an area for playing certain sports
examples: The case went to court and the judge found him not guilty.

covered.simple_definition: having something placed on top; included in an agreement

cream.simple_definition: a thick white liquid from milk; a soft substance used on the skin

cruel.simple_definition: causing pain or suffering on purpose; very unkind

cultural.simple_definition: relating to the culture, arts, and traditions of a society
examples: The city hosts a large cultural festival every summer.

currency.simple_definition: the system of money used in a country

current.simple_definition: happening or existing now; a steady flow of water or electricity

The position is currently open and we are accepting applications this month.

curtain.simple_definition: a piece of cloth that hangs in front of a window or on a stage
examples: She pulled the curtains open in the morning to let the sunlight in.

custom.simple_definition: a traditional way of behaving common in a society
examples: It is a local custom to give gifts to neighbours during the harvest festival.

cut.simple_definition: to use a sharp tool to divide something; an injury made by something sharp
examples: She cut the bread into slices and put them on the table.

damage.simple_definition: harm or injury caused to something; to cause harm
examples: The flooding caused significant damage to homes in the lower part of town.
The city has changed enormously over the past decade.

decorate.simple_definition: to make something look more attractive by adding things to it
examples: They decorated the hall with lights and flowers for the evening celebration.

define.simple_definition: to explain the meaning of a word or concept; to describe clearly
examples: The word justice is difficult to define because people see it differently.

definite.simple_definition: clear and certain; not in doubt
examples: There is no definite answer — it depends on the situation.

definition.simple_definition: an explanation of the meaning of a word or phrase
examples: Look up the definition of that word in the dictionary if you are not sure.

deliver.simple_definition: to take goods or letters to a place; to give a speech
examples: The postman delivered a large parcel to the house early in the morning.

departure.simple_definition: the act of leaving a place; the time a plane or train leaves
examples: Please arrive at the airport at least two hours before your departure.

despite.simple_definition: used to say something happens even though something else might prevent it

destination.simple_definition: the place you are travelling to

determine.simple_definition: to decide something firmly; to be the main cause of something
examples: The results will help us determine the best course of treatment.

determined.simple_definition: having a firm decision to do something; not giving up
examples: She was absolutely determined to finish the race, even when it got hard.

development.simple_definition: the process of growing or changing into something more advanced
examples: There have been exciting new developments in the field of medical research.

diagram.simple_definition: a simple picture or drawing that explains how something works
examples: The teacher drew a diagram to show how the water cycle works.

diamond.simple_definition: a very hard, clear precious stone; a shape with four equal sides
examples: She wore a ring with a small diamond on her right hand.

difficulty.simple_definition: a problem that makes something hard to do
examples: The main difficulty with the plan is that it will take too long to complete.

directly.simple_definition: in a straight line; immediately; with nothing in between

dirt.simple_definition: material such as mud or dust that makes something unclean

disadvantage.simple_definition: something that makes a situation harder or less favourable

disappointed.simple_definition: feeling unhappy because something did not happen as you hoped
examples: He was very disappointed when he found out he had not got the job.

disappointing.simple_definition: causing a feeling of disappointment; not as good as hoped
examples: The film had a lot of promise but the ending was quite disappointing.

discount.simple_definition: a reduction in the usual price of something

dislike.simple_definition: a feeling of not liking something; to not like something
examples: She has a strong dislike of loud music, so she avoids crowded venues.

divide.simple_definition: to separate into parts; to share among a group

documentary.simple_definition: a film or programme about real events or people
examples: The documentary explored the lives of people in a remote mountain village.

donate.simple_definition: to give something, especially money, to an organisation or good cause
examples: The company decided to donate ten percent of its profits to local charities.

doubt.simple_definition: a feeling of uncertainty; to be uncertain about something
examples: I have no doubt that she will do an excellent job in her new role.

dressed.simple_definition: wearing clothes; wearing clothes of a particular type

drum.simple_definition: a musical instrument that is hit to make a sound; to tap something repeatedly
examples: He has been playing the drums in the school band for over three years.

drunk.simple_definition: having had too much alcohol; not in control because of alcohol

due.simple_definition: expected or planned for a certain time; owed to someone

dust.simple_definition: tiny dry particles that settle on surfaces; to clean dust from something

duty.simple_definition: something you must do because of your job or moral responsibility

earthquake.simple_definition: a sudden shaking of the ground caused by movement in the earth's crust

eastern.simple_definition: in or from the east part of a country, region, or the world

economic.simple_definition: relating to the economy or the management of money
examples: The government introduced new economic measures to reduce unemployment.

economy.simple_definition: the system of money, trade, and industry in a country
examples: The economy grew strongly last year due to increased exports.

edge.simple_definition: the outside limit of something; the sharp side of a blade
examples: Be careful near the edge of the cliff — the path is very narrow there.

editor.simple_definition: a person who decides what is included in a newspaper, book, or film
examples: The editor of the newspaper decided not to publish the controversial article.

educate.simple_definition: to teach someone, especially as part of a school or training programme
examples: The goal is to educate young people about environmental issues.

educated.simple_definition: having received a good education; having knowledge through study
examples: She is a well-educated woman who speaks four languages fluently.

educational.simple_definition: providing knowledge or information; related to education
examples: The visit to the science museum was a very educational experience for the children.

effective.simple_definition: producing the result you want; working well

effectively.simple_definition: in a way that produces the results you want

effort.simple_definition: physical or mental energy used to do something; a serious attempt

election.simple_definition: the process of choosing a person or group by voting
examples: The general election will take place next spring and both parties are campaigning.

element.simple_definition: a basic or essential part of something; a chemical substance

embarrassed.simple_definition: feeling ashamed or awkward about something

embarrassing.simple_definition: causing feelings of shame or awkwardness
examples: It was embarrassing to arrive late and find everyone had already sat down.

emergency.simple_definition: a sudden serious situation requiring immediate action

emotion.simple_definition: a strong feeling such as joy, love, fear, or anger

employment.simple_definition: the state of having a paid job; the number of people who have work

encourage.simple_definition: to give someone confidence or support to do something
examples: Her teacher encouraged her to enter the national writing competition.

enemy.simple_definition: a person who hates and wants to harm you; the opposite of a friend

engaged.simple_definition: having agreed to get married; busy or occupied

engineering.simple_definition: the profession of designing and building machines, roads, and structures
examples: He studied civil engineering and now works on large construction projects.

entertain.simple_definition: to keep someone amused and interested; to consider an idea
examples: The speaker managed to entertain the audience while giving useful information.

entertainment.simple_definition: something that gives people pleasure, such as films, music, or shows

entrance.simple_definition: the door or opening you go through to enter a place

entry.simple_definition: the act of going into a place; the right to enter a place or competition

environmental.simple_definition: related to the natural world and the impact people have on it

episode.simple_definition: a single part of a television series; an event in someone's life

equal.simple_definition: the same in size, amount, or value; treating everyone the same way

equally.simple_definition: in the same way; to the same degree

survivor.simple_definition: a person who continues to live after an event or illness

examples: We used "receiver" in Bogazici - Law coursework.

receivership.simple_definition: A court action that places property under the control of a receiver during litigation so that it can be preserved for the benefit of all
examples: We used "receivership" in Bogazici - Law coursework.
